title: [bug]: Calendar Range/Multiselect: Third click doesn't properly reset start date in range selection
author: matinsekhavat
body:
### Describe the bug

### Description

In the shadcn/ui Calendar component (especially with `mode="range"`), the range selection behavior is unintuitive on subsequent selections.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Use `<Calendar mode="range" ... />`
2. Click a start date (first click).
3. Click an end date (second click) → range is correctly selected.
4. Click a new date to begin a fresh range (third click).

**Expected behavior:**
- The third click should **reset** the previous range and set the clicked date as the **new start date**.

**Actual behavior:**
- It sticks to the original start date. The third click does not reset the range properly and doesn't treat the new date as the new origin.

### Suggested improvement

- When a range is already selected, the next single click should automatically clear the previous range and start a new one from that date (standard UX in most date pickers).
- Alternatively, expose a prop like `resetOnNewSelection={true}` (default `true`) to control this behavior.

### Additional info

- This makes quickly selecting multiple different ranges feel broken.
- Built on top of `react-day-picker`.


---

This small UX fix would make the range picker much more user-friendly. Thanks!

### Affected component/components

calendar

### How to reproduce

go inside main calendar shadcn samples u can test

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
browsers
```

title: [bug]: Calendar remounts its entire DOM on every re-render — inline Root/Chevron/WeekNumber components create new component types each render
author: fm1cc
body:
### Describe the bug

The `calendar.tsx` template defines three custom components **inline inside JSX** ([current main, lines 135–166](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/calendar.tsx)):

```tsx
components={{
  Root: ({ className, rootRef, ...props }) => { ... },   // new function identity every render
  Chevron: ({ className, orientation, ...props }) => { ... },
  DayButton: CalendarDayButton,
  WeekNumber: ({ children, ...props }) => { ... },
  ...components,
}}
```

react-day-picker v9/v10 renders these directly as JSX element types (`React.createElement(components.Root, ...)` in `DayPicker.js`), and its internal `useMemo` depends on `props.components` — which is a fresh object literal on every render, so the memo never holds. New arrow function each render → new component type → React unmounts and remounts the **entire calendar subtree** on every re-render of `Calendar`.

This violates the React rule against defining components during render, and it stays invisible until a parent re-renders the calendar frequently — e.g. a hotel-search style range picker that stores a `hovered` day in parent state for range preview: every `onDayMouseEnter` tears down and rebuilds the whole calendar DOM.

**Consequences observed:**

- Full DOM teardown/rebuild per parent re-render (performance)
- Keyboard focus loss / `CalendarDayButton`'s `useEffect` re-focusing on every remount
- **macOS Safari, `mode="range"` inside a Popover: after picking the start date, clicks on the end date are silently swallowed** (hover preview still renders, click does nothing). Safari never mouse-focuses buttons and blurs the programmatically-focused day on `mousedown`; with the calendar remounting between events, the `mousedown`/`click` targets no longer match and the click is lost. Hoisting the three components to module scope made this bug disappear — likely related to older Safari reports like #3516 and PR #3066 that were addressed at the symptom level

**Suggested fix** — hoist the three inline components to module scope (same pattern already used for `CalendarDayButton`):

```tsx
const CalendarRoot: CustomComponents["Root"] = ({ className, rootRef, ...props }) => (
  <div data-slot="calendar" ref={rootRef} className={cn(className)} {...props} />
)
// ... CalendarChevron, CalendarWeekNumber likewise

components={{
  Root: CalendarRoot,
  Chevron: CalendarChevron,
  DayButton: CalendarDayButton,
  WeekNumber: CalendarWeekNumber,
  ...components,
}}
```

No visual or API change; identity becomes stable and remounts stop.

### Affected component/components

Calendar (and Date Picker, which composes it)

### How to reproduce

1. Add the stock `calendar.tsx` to a project
2. Add a mount log inside any of the inline components:

```tsx
Root: ({ className, rootRef, ...props }) => {
  React.useEffect(() => console.log("Calendar Root mounted"), [])
  return <div ref={rootRef} className={cn(className)} {...props} />
}
```

3. Render `<Calendar mode="range" ... />` and trigger any parent state change on each re-render (e.g. store a hovered day in parent state via `onDayMouseEnter` for range preview)
4. Observe "Calendar Root mounted" logged on **every** parent re-render — the whole calendar DOM is torn down and rebuilt each time
5. (Safari-specific symptom) On macOS Safari, put the range Calendar inside a Popover with the hover-preview pattern above: after selecting the start date, clicks on the end date are swallowed. Hoist the three components to module scope and the clicks work again

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash
# console output while moving the mouse across days (hover state in parent):
Calendar Root mounted
Calendar Root mounted
Calendar Root mounted
# ... one full remount per re-render
```

### System Info

```bash
- OS/Browser: macOS Safari for the click-loss symptom; the remount itself reproduces on any browser/OS
- React: 19.1
- react-day-picker: 10.0.1
- radix-ui: 1.6.1
- Vite: 8.1, TypeScript 6.0, Node 24
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues

title: [bug]: `MessageScroller`: mount-time anchors are never registered, so a same-count content swap re-anchors to a stale message
author: medihack

title: [bug]: `MessageScroller`: a manual scroll during an anchored streaming turn destroys the reserved tail spacer
author: alvarosevilla95
body:
### Describe the bug

With `autoScroll` + `scrollAnchor` (the setup in the `message-scroller-streaming` example), a new turn anchors near the top and the reserved **tail spacer** is consumed correctly as the reply streams — **until the reader scrolls**. A manual scroll during the anchored (pre-handoff) phase releases the anchor, and the spacer is then never reconciled again. What happens next depends on `autoScroll`:

- **`autoScroll` on (the streaming example's config):** the scroll releases the anchor, follow-mode re-arms, and `scrollToEnd` zeroes the spacer in a single frame → the reserved room **collapses** and the viewport is yanked to the live edge, even when the reply is still small.
- **`autoScroll` off:** nothing re-arms follow, so the spacer **freezes** at its current height → a viewport of dead space is stranded below the reply and never resolves.

Either way, a normal reader action (scrolling while a reply streams) destroys the reserved room.

### Affected component/components

MessageScroller

### How to reproduce

1. `MessageScrollerProvider autoScroll` + `scrollAnchor={message.role === "user"}`
   (the streaming example).
2. Send a message; a user turn anchors near the top with room reserved below.
3. While the assistant reply streams, scroll up a little (or down toward the bottom).

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

https://stackblitz.com/github/alvarosevilla95/message-scroller-repro

### System Info

```bash
Chrome 150.0.7871.115
Firefox 152.0.4
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues

title: [bug]: Init preset overwrites font mapping but leaves default GeistSans imported as dead weight
author: Sabourifar
body:
### Describe the bug

When initializing a fresh Next.js project with the --preset b8B4SWmbGE --pointer flags, the preset adds Inter and EB_Garamond as requested, but fails to remove the default Geist font from the generated layout.tsx. The preset also rewrites the --font-sans CSS mapping, making the imported Geist font completely unused, causing unnecessary download overhead.

### Affected component/components

layout.tsx, globals.css

### How to reproduce

1. Create a fresh Next.js app: bun create next-app@latest (Accept all defaults)
2. Run the shadcn init preset: bunx --bun shadcn@latest init --preset b8B4SWmbGE --pointer
3. Open the generated app/layout.tsx and app/globals.css.

title: [bug]: MessageScroller breaks inside iframe portals — cross-realm `instanceof HTMLElement` in geometry
author: marcandreh
body:
### Describe the bug

`@shadcn/react/message-scroller` stops tracking the scroll edge when the scroller is rendered into a **same-origin iframe** via `createPortal` (a common pattern for style-isolated embeddable widgets).

Root cause: `getMessageScrollerItems` in `packages/react/src/message-scroller/geometry.ts` filters content children with

```ts
return Array.from(content.children).filter(
  (child): child is HTMLElement =>
    child instanceof HTMLElement && child !== spacer
)
```

When the DOM lives in an iframe, the elements are instances of the **iframe realm's** `HTMLElement`, not the parent realm's, so `child instanceof HTMLElement` is `false` for every child (classic cross-realm `instanceof` pitfall). The items list is always empty, which cascades:

- The effective content height (`getEffectiveScrollHeight`) reduces to the content padding only, so `scrollable.end` is **always `false`** no matter how far up the reader scrolls.
- `MessageScrollerButton` never activates (`useMessageScrollerScrollable` is permanently stale).
- `reconcileFollowMode` sees `autoScroll && !scrollable.end` on every state commit and **re-arms `following-bottom`**, so a user wheel/touch scroll away from the bottom is immediately re-stuck to the live edge on the next content growth — the "never move the reader against their intent" guarantee inverts.
- Anchor/turn positioning and ResizeObserver-driven following still work (they don't go through the `instanceof` filter), which makes the failure look mysterious: streaming follows fine, but release + the scroll button are dead.

Everything works correctly the moment the same tree renders in the top-level document.

**Suggested fix** (happy to submit a PR): replace the realm-sensitive check with a realm-safe one, e.g.

```ts
child.nodeType === Node.ELEMENT_NODE && child !== spacer
```

or, to keep the HTML-only semantics: `child instanceof (child.ownerDocument.defaultView ?? window).HTMLElement`. This is the only `instanceof HTMLElement` in the package, so it's a one-line fix. We're currently shipping exactly that as a `pnpm patch` of `@shadcn/react@0.2.0` in production and it fully restores button activation and follow-release inside the iframe.

### Affected component/components

MessageScroller (`@shadcn/react/message-scroller`)

### How to reproduce

1. Create an iframe in the page and grab its body (same origin): `iframe.contentDocument.body`.
2. Render the standard MessageScroller tree from the docs into it with `createPortal(<MessageScrollerProvider autoScroll>…</MessageScrollerProvider>, iframeBody)`, with enough items to overflow the viewport.
3. Scroll the viewport up with the mouse wheel.
4. Observe: `data-scrollable` on the viewport never contains `"end"`, `MessageScrollerButton` stays `data-active="false"`/inert, and while content is growing the viewport snaps back to the bottom despite the user's scroll.
5. Move the identical tree out of the iframe into the main document — everything behaves correctly.

Minimal repro sketch:

```tsx
function IframePortal({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [body, setBody] = React.useState<HTMLElement | null>(null)
  return (
    <iframe ref={(el) => setBody(el?.contentDocument?.body ?? null)}>
      {body && createPortal(children, body)}
    </iframe>
  )
}
// <IframePortal>{docs MessageScroller example}</IframePortal>
```

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_Not provided — the root cause is a single realm-sensitive line, cited above with the exact file and behavior; the sketch reproduces it verbatim. Can add a sandbox if needed._

### Logs

_No errors are thrown — the `instanceof` filter fails silently, which is what makes this hard to diagnose._

### System Info

```
@shadcn/react 0.2.0, React 18.3.1, Chromium/Firefox/WebKit (all reproduce)
```

title: [bug]: `SidebarProvider` registers a global keyboard shortcut for toggling the sidebar with `Cmd`+`B` / `Ctrl`+`B`
author: NuclEnergy
body:
### Describe the bug

### Description

`SidebarProvider` registers a global keyboard shortcut for toggling the sidebar with `Cmd`+`B` / `Ctrl`+`B`.

Current matching logic only checks:

```ts
event.key === "b" && (event.metaKey || event.ctrlKey);
```

It does not exclude additional modifier keys such as `Shift` or `Alt`.

As a result, `Cmd`+`Shift`+`B`, which is a browser-level shortcut, can be intercepted by the sidebar shortcut
handler in some environments. The handler then calls event.preventDefault(), preventing the browser
default behavior from working.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a page using SidebarProvider.
2. Press `Cmd`+`B`.
3. Sidebar toggles correctly.
4. Press `Cmd`+`Shift`+`B`.
5. Browser default behavior does not work as expected.

### Expected Behavior

Only plain `Cmd`+`B` / `Ctrl`+`B` should toggle the sidebar.

Cmd+Shift+B should be left untouched so the browser can handle its default behavior.

### Actual Behavior

`Cmd`+`Shift`+`B` may be captured by the sidebar shortcut handler and blocked by `event.preventDefault()`.

### Suspected Cause

The shortcut check does not require an exact modifier match.

### Suggested Fix

Require the shortcut to be plain `Cmd`/`Ctrl`+`B` and ignore combinations with extra modifiers:

```ts
if (
  event.key.toLowerCase() === SIDEBAR_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUT &&
  (event.metaKey || event.ctrlKey) &&
  !event.shiftKey &&
  !event.altKey
) {
  event.preventDefault();
  toggleSidebar();
}
```


### Affected component/components

Sidebar

### How to reproduce

1. Open a page using SidebarProvider.
2. Press `Cmd`+`B`.
3. Sidebar toggles correctly.
4. Press `Cmd`+`Shift`+`B`.
5. Browser default behavior does not work as expected.

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
Chip: M3 Ultra 512GB+16TB
System: macOS Tahoe 26.5.1
Browser: Chrome Version 149.0.7827.201 (Official Build) (arm64)
```

title: [bug]: data-selected:* variant is incorrect for Tailwind v4 Command component
author: ariannargesi
body:
### Describe the bug

## Describe the bug

The `Command` component documentation uses the following Tailwind variants:

```tsx
data-selected:bg-muted
data-selected:text-foreground
```

However, these variants are not recognized by Tailwind.

The correct syntax is:

```tsx
data-[selected]:bg-muted
data-[selected]:text-foreground
```

Without this change, the selected item styles are never applied.

## Expected behavior

The documentation should use:

```tsx
data-[selected]:bg-muted
data-[selected]:text-foreground
```

instead of:

```tsx
data-selected:bg-muted
data-selected:text-foreground
```

## Additional context

I noticed this in the `Command` component documentation. It appears to be a typo in the documented Tailwind variant rather than an issue with the component itself.

### Affected component/components

Command

### How to reproduce


## Reproduction

1. Copy the `Command` component from the documentation.
2. Use the documented `data-selected:*` classes.
3. Notice that the selected state receives the `data-selected` attribute, but the styles are not applied.
4. Replace the classes with `data-[selected]:*`.
5. The selected styles now work correctly.

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### System Info

```bash
MacOS, chrome browser
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues

title: [bug]: select/combobox (or similar) doesn't render options
author: simonglob
body:
### Describe the bug

I have been trying to add a combobox to my site, but it doesn't render the options, so I tried with a select component instead and same thing happened. 

Here is my example.
```typescript
"use client";

import { Select, SelectContent, SelectGroup, SelectItem, SelectLabel, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from "~/components/ui/select";

export default function SelectTest() {
  const items = [{
    label: "foo",
    value: "0"
  }, {
    label: "bar",
    value: "1"
  }];
  return (
    <Select>
      <SelectTrigger className="w-full max-w-48">
        <SelectValue />
      </SelectTrigger>
      <SelectContent>
        <SelectGroup>
          <SelectLabel>Stuff</SelectLabel>
          {items.map((item) => (
            <SelectItem key={item.value} value={item.value}>
              {item.label}
            </SelectItem>
          ))}
        </SelectGroup>
      </SelectContent>
    </Select>
  );
}
```

It doesn't allow me to interact with the select box at all. For combobox, I can write in the input, but still no options are being rendered

title: [bug]: MessageScroller fires "ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications" during streamed content growth
author: devnomic
body:
### Describe the bug

`MessageScroller` (from `@shadcn/react`) fires the window error event `ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications` whenever streamed content grows while the scroller is auto-following the bottom or re-anchoring, e.g. an AI chat streaming an assistant reply with `scrollAnchor` user messages. Dev tooling that surfaces window errors (Vite overlay, Sentry, console "Uncaught") reports it on every streamed turn.

### Root cause

Both `MessageScrollerViewport` and `MessageScrollerContent` observe their elements with a `ResizeObserver` whose callback is the controller's `handleResize`, invoked synchronously during observation delivery:

https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/packages/react/src/message-scroller/components.tsx (the two `new ResizeObserver(handleResize)` effects)

`handleResize` synchronously calls `scrollToEnd()` / `reanchorToAnchoredMessage()`, and both paths mutate the spacer height (`setSpacerHeight`). The spacer div lives inside the observed content element, so the observed element is resized again in the same frame, during observer delivery. Per the ResizeObserver spec's loop-limiting algorithm, the browser must then fire the `error` event "ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications" at `window` and defer delivery to the next frame.

Sequence during streaming:

1. Streamed tokens grow the content element, the content `ResizeObserver` fires.
2. `handleResize` runs inside delivery and collapses/sets the spacer height (for example transitioning from an anchored message to bottom-following).
3. The observed content element has resized again in the same frame, so the browser reports undelivered notifications.

### Suggested fix

Coalesce the resize handling into `requestAnimationFrame` (the same approach floating-ui uses), so the scroll/spacer mutations happen outside observer delivery:

```tsx
let frame = 0

const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
  window.cancelAnimationFrame(frame)
  frame = window.requestAnimationFrame(handleResize)
})
```

The behavior is unchanged (one frame of deferral at most) and the error event no longer fires. I have a PR ready and will link it here.

### Affected component/components

MessageScroller

### How to reproduce

1. Render `MessageScroller` with `autoScroll` and items using `scrollAnchor` (the AI chat / anchoring pattern from the docs).
2. Append streamed chunks to the last message (fast interval or a real LLM stream).
3. Watch the browser console / window `error` events: `ResizeObserver loop completed with undelivered notifications` fires repeatedly while the reply streams.

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs


title: [feat]: Auto Message Scroller
author: krup003
body:
### Feature description

Recently you added a component called Message Scroller. It's a great component from my perspective, but right now, as developers, many of us need autoscroll functionality, and there isn't a proper package for it on npm.

Could you please add autoscroll to your Message Scroller component?

It should stop auto-scrolling when the user interacts (e.g., scrolls up), and resume auto-scrolling once the user scrolls back to the bottom.

I appreciate your attention.

Thank you.
@shadcn 

### Affected component/components

Message Scroller

### Additional Context

It should stop auto-scrolling when the user interacts (e.g., scrolls up), and resume auto-scrolling once the user scrolls back to the bottom.

title: Registry auth fails on non-www redirects since node-fetch was replaced in CLI 4.11.1+
author: JugglerX
body:
### Describe the bug

This is not stricly a bug. But I believe its something that will effect many registries.

# Registry auth fails on non-`www`/cross-host redirects since `node-fetch` was replaced with native `fetch` (4.11.1+)

## Summary

The latest shadcn CLI 4.11.1+ switched from `node-fetch` to native `fetch`. Now `shadcn add` returns `401 Unauthorized` for authenticated registries whose URL redirects across hosts (e.g. apex → `www`), **even with a valid API key**. Pointing the registry at the post-redirect (canonical) host works.

**For example:** Prior to this change our registry worked with both www and non-www urls https://www.shadcnblocks.com/r/{name} or https://shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}. After this change the non-www version no longer works.

```
{
  "registries": {
    "@shadcnblocks": {
      "url": "https://www.shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ${SHADCNBLOCKS_API_KEY}" }
    }
  }
}
```

## Root cause

When a registry URL responds with a redirect to a **different host** (e.g. `https://example.com/r/{name}` → `https://www.example.com/r/{name}`), native `fetch`/undici **drops the `Authorization` header** on the cross-origin hop for security. The token never reaches the final host → `401`.

## `node-fetch` → native `fetch`

This changed behavior in **`4.11.1`** (commit `9197676`, "chore: replace node-fetch", 2026-06-15), which removed `node-fetch`/`https-proxy-agent` in favor of native `fetch` + `undici`.

- `node-fetch` used a lenient rule: it kept `Authorization` when the redirect target was the **same domain or a subdomain** (so apex → `www` preserved the header).
- Native `fetch`/undici strips `Authorization` on **any cross-origin redirect**, including apex ↔ `www`.

| CLI version | fetch impl | cross-host redirect w/ auth |
|---|---|---|
| ≤ 4.11.0 | node-fetch | header preserved (same-domain/subdomain) |
| 4.11.1+ / current | native fetch + undici | **header stripped → 401** |

Because `npx shadcn` (unpinned) resolves to the latest, configs that worked before now fail after a routine upgrade — with no change on the user's side.

## Reproduction

1. Configure an authenticated registry whose URL 301/308-redirects to another host (e.g. apex → `www`):

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "@shadcnblocks": {
      "url": "https://shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ${SHADCNBLOCKS_API_KEY}" }
    }
  }
}
```

2. Both `npx shadcn@latest add @shadcnblocks/hero36` or `npx shadcn@4.11.1 add @shadcnblocks/hero36` will fail with `401`
3. Change the URL to the redirect target host (`https://www.shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}`) → succeeds.

Alternatively if you a cli version before 4.11.1 it will work with both www and non-ww versions

1. make sure the url is non-www https://shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}
2. `npx shadcn@4.11.0  add @shadcnblocks/hero36` will work


## Verified behavior (same cross-host 308)

| Client | Keeps `Authorization`? |
|---|---|
| `node-fetch` (≤ 4.11.0) | ✅ kept on same-domain/subdomain |
| native `fetch` / undici (4.11.1+) | ❌ stripped |
| `curl -L` | ❌ stripped |

## Impact

- Any registry that relies on a host-level redirect (apex → `www` is extremely common on Vercel/Netlify/Cloudflare) breaks for authenticated installs on current CLI.
- Also affects `bunx`, Deno, and CI, which strip cross-host auth regardless of version.

## Immediate Mitigations

- **Registry operators:** publish the canonical (post-redirect) host in `components.json`
-  Avoid cross-host redirects on registry paths.

## Possible fix

While this is likely expected native `fetch` behavior, the CLI could handle this case for a better ongoing DX.

### Option 1: Document the caveat (lowest effort)

In registry/auth docs, note that:

- registry URLs should use the **canonical URL** (final URL after redirects)
- auth headers from `registries.*.headers` are sent on the initial request only
- they are **not** re-sent across origin-changing redirects

### Option 2: CLI warning (helpful, still spec-correct)

If a registry request fails with 401/403 and the response was reached via a redirect that changed host/scheme/port, emit a targeted message:

> Auth headers were not sent to the redirected URL (`www.example.com`). Use the canonical registry URL in `components.json`.

### Option 3: CLI compatibility layer (optional)

To restore prior `node-fetch` ergonomics for private registries:

- detect redirects where the target is the **same registrable domain** (e.g. `example.com` ↔ `www.example.com`)
- manually follow the redirect and **re-attach configured per-registry headers** on the redirected request

This would be an intentional deviation from strict fetch redirect semantics, so it should probably be:

- opt-in (e.g. `followRegistryRedirects: true`), or
- limited to same-site/subdomain cases only


### Affected component/components

Registry

### How to reproduce


1. Configure an authenticated registry whose URL 301/308-redirects to another host (e.g. apex → `www`):

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "@shadcnblocks": {
      "url": "https://shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ${SHADCNBLOCKS_API_KEY}" }
    }
  }
}
```

2. Both `npx shadcn@latest add @shadcnblocks/hero36` or `npx shadcn@4.11.1 add @shadcnblocks/hero36` will fail with `401`
3. Change the URL to the redirect target host (`https://www.shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}`) → succeeds.

Alternatively if you a cli version before 4.11.1 it will work with both www and non-ww versions

1. make sure the url is non-www https://shadcnblocks.com/r/{name}
2. `npx shadcn@4.11.0  add @shadcnblocks/hero36` will work

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
Node v22.15.0
```

- [x] I've searched for existing issues

title: [bug]: autoScroll behavior on message-scroller
author: achristie
body:
### Describe the bug

i think it's a bug anyway. Basically if i set autoScroll on the `MessageScrollerProvider` then it no longer sends a new message to the top of the viewport. I want both behaviors - new message goes to top of view and, unless the user intervenes, stream follows the live edge.

### Affected component/components

message-scroller

### How to reproduce

new message goes to top of viewport, but it will no longer treat the chat as being at the live edge.
```jsx
<MessageScrollerProvider
      defaultScrollPosition="end"
      scrollPreviousItemPeek={0}
      scrollMargin={8}
    >
      <Chat {...props} />
</MessageScrollerProvider>
```

new message does not go to top of viewport, but follows the stream at the live edge.
```jsx
<MessageScrollerProvider
      autoScroll
      defaultScrollPosition="end"
      scrollPreviousItemPeek={0}
      scrollMargin={8}
    >
      <Chat {...props} />
</MessageScrollerProvider>
```


### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
mac, chrome, "@shadcn/react": "^0.2.0"
```

- [x] I've searched for existing issues
